title: Hattie's Shell Necklace Adventure
summary: At three in the morning, Hattie the Mouse and her many friends—including Mrs. Strawberry, Victor, and Habattaly—wake up at Hattie's house for a day of play. After a hearty breakfast, the group sets out on a walk through a sunlit field to a nearby pond. At the water's edge, they discover a collection of unique shells and decide to string them together into a beautiful necklace, which Lily eventually wears. Their journey continues deep into a quiet, cool forest and eventually leads them to a majestic canyon, where a gentle stream flows between steep rocky cliffs.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. Now I'm going to turn on the lights and we're all ready for Stars and Story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stars. Stars. The moon. Stars. Just stars. But there's no stories!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well we better tell a story, huh?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Always about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Always about Hattie. And no knocks.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, no one knocked on the door. What kind of story should we tell tonight then?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: At three is morning time, so everyone woke up.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who woke up?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie, Mrs. Strawberry, Victor, Victor Too, Yahoo, Habattaly!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what did they all do together this morning?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They played. They played.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They played? Oh I forgot to set the timer. Where's the timer? 35... let's see.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, where did you left that timer?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's a good question, Izzy. Oh, it was under the lion's stool. All right, I'll set the timer. Okay, now we're all ready to go.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Go go go!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So everyone woke up in the morning at Hattie the Mouse's house. And what did they all do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They played, they played, they played. They played and played. They played and played and played so much.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow. What kinds of things were they playing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad sit up because these ones want to go on your legs.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Hello Nana's Bunny. Hello ER. And who's this? Oh, hello Oscar. And Pooky would like to sit... Oh hi Pooky. You can sit in my lap too.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad can you please tell the story for me? I don't have any stories about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, everyone had breakfast at Hattie the Mouse's house. And then they decided to go out for a walk. And since it was such a beautiful day out, they set out for their walk out the back door of Hattie the Mouse's house and right out into the field. And they walked through the field and at the edge of the field, there was a beautiful little pond. And they walked to the edge of the pond and there they saw something very interesting. What did they see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some shells!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Some shells? Oh, what kind of shells?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, they found some shells and they decided to collect them. And they thought, hmm, maybe we could do a crafts activity with the shells and make something beautiful. So what did they decide to make?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I use these pillows how they walk.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh you're walking with the pillows.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I also used to gallop and jump and march and prance. Wow, I also go upside down and jump my feet upside down. And I also use them for when we walk and I also use them to do my freeze dances.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Your freeze dances? Wow, that sounds like fun.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and I'm going to show you them in the morning when I wake up. But I'm going to do one tonight.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Show me, show me.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: These are one of my freeze dances. Oh, you're jumping with your feet. Haha! Exactly! And one is hop spin twirler. Hop spin twirler. Hop spin twirler. That's hop spin twirler. Jump back! Jump back! Jump back! Oops!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No stars anymore.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oops, the lantern booped over. I'll fix it. There. Now let's lie down for Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, we can lie down for Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they decided to make a beautiful necklace out of all the shells. Each shell had a little hole in it already. And they went and found a nice piece of string and they strung the shells along the string. One, two, three, four, five beautiful shells.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And there were one, two, three, four, ten!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So there were ten shells.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And there was a zero that had a zero hole in it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A zero hole?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They snugged and wore the necklace. And everybody wanted to wear the necklace.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Everybody wanted to wear the necklace. So who got to wear it in the end?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Lily.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh no, oh... ball! That's okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The tall house! Yeah, the tall house came down too. Dad, can you fix it for me?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, I can fix it, don't worry. That was a very tall house. Are you done pooping do you think?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I am. I am gone too! Diaper, diaper, sniper.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Diaper, diaper, sniper? Yes, we're going to change you pretty soon.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I want to change my diaper right now.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh you do want to change it now? Okay, we can do that.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Poop is yucky.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's certainly true. I agree. It's yucky.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want to go on the peanut back lane.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You're on the peanut back lane. Like that? I don't think we can do it like that, no.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And can you do it... can you do it like this? And or can you do it like this? No. How can we do it like this?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Let's see. Can we do it like this? Yes, we can do it just like that. Like this? Well, if you cross your legs it'll be harder to change you actually. Can I do a new way to change? I think we should do the usual way to change. Like this. Okay, there we go.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't want to be in my poopy.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No you don't want to be in your poopy diaper. Who wants to be in a poopy diaper?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No one. Oscar likes to do yucky and junky stuff.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He does? I didn't know that. Why does he like that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He eats it! Like that. ABCDEFGHIJK...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad can you please keep telling the story while you're changing my diaper?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yeah, that's a challenge. I think I could. So, after they made a beautiful necklace out of shells sitting at the edge of the pond, they decided to keep going on their walk. And so they continued past the pond into the forest. And as they got further and further into the forest, they realized they were in a part of the forest called the deep dark forest. It wasn't scary, it was just really quiet in there. And there were so many trees and so many leaves that the sunlight couldn't really reach to the ground. And so it was a little darker than the other parts of the forest inside there. And it was actually kind of nice and it was cool.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Dad, they don't need to change my shirt because it didn't get dirty.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You want to sleep in that shirt you think? Yep. Okay. Let me just check and make sure I got you totally clean. I think so. Okay, good enough. You look very clean.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, can you please keep doing the forest part?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah. In the deep dark forest, they saw lots of beautiful things that lived only there in that special dark part of the forest.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then they went to the canyon!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The canyon!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh the canyon. They stayed there... They went to the canyon. The canyon is a place between two steep cliffs of rock. And inside the canyon, there was a little stream running. And it made a trickling sound... trickle-trickle-trickle-trickle. And there were little trees and bushes growing all along the sides. And birds flying in and out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. Now it's time to turn off the stars.

characters.name: Hattie
characters.description: A clever mouse milliner and engineer of whimsy, she is a resourceful spirit who crafts exquisite hats and soulful portraits within her hollow tree stump workshop. Though a formidable presence famously counted among the thirty legendary cats, she is currently confined to her bed by illness, her usual treks through saturated woodlands for rare tape and lollipops temporarily halted. Even in her malaise, her sharp mind lingers on a recent, stinging disagreement with Dimitar over a fish, a rare shadow cast upon a heart otherwise known for its boundless generosity and creative light.

characters.name: Mrs. Strawberry
characters.description: One of Hattie's friends who joins the morning walk.

characters.name: Victor
characters.description: Clad in blue shorts and a purple shirt, Lily’s brother is a tiny baby mouse and spirited cosmic stowaway who once served as a royal gardener within the Magic Forest’s glitter-shimmering houses. Now a rugged driver of a tow truck in the big car race, he balances his mechanical duties with a quest for a celestial watering can and the composition of percussive ‘bamp-bam-bum’ beats for space-faring worms. Though he treasures the sparkly art of Uncle Grisco and Haddy, his high-speed adventures often result in muddy messes that necessitate restorative baths, leaving him in singular pursuit of the perfect hat to complete his interstellar ensemble.

characters.name: Victor Too
characters.description: Another companion in Hattie's group of friends.

characters.name: Yahoo
characters.description: A member of the group who wakes up at three in the morning.

characters.name: Habattaly
characters.description: One of the many friends gathered at Hattie's house.

characters.name: Nana's Bunny
characters.description: A stuffed toy present during the storytelling.

characters.name: ER
characters.description: One of the toys sitting on Dad's legs.

characters.name: Oscar
characters.description: Hunkered in velvety shadows beside one of Mistress Mouse’s tattered stuffy mice, Lily’s brother illuminates a salvaged television with a scavenged flashlight, contentedly nibbling on oily debris. His focus drifts from the flickering screen to the miniature green car mired in a mud puddle—a casualty of the same reckless speed he employs when racing his yellow school bus. Between these high-stakes heats, he engineers sturdy Lego bridges that arch over the surrounding wreckage, all under the unblinking watch of his glass-eyed, plush sentinel.

characters.name: Pooky
characters.description: A pig stuffie who sits in Dad's lap during the story.

characters.name: Lily
characters.description: The world’s tallest mouse is a guardian scholar of monumental power and studious grace, serving as the living pillar of a communal house where he hoists the massive roof with peerless strength. Between shifts at the Pepperoni Street tape store and classes at the local school, this friend of Mistress Mouse finds quiet joy building with Legos or coloring in the baby lounge alongside his many brothers. Adorned in his signature shell necklace and a fresh bespoke hat from Haddy, he remains a gentle soul whose immense physical duty is matched only by his dedicated, playful heart.

places.name: Hattie the Mouse's House
places.description: The starting point of the adventure where everyone has breakfast.

places.name: The Field
places.description: Behind Hattie’s house lies a sprawling, sun-drenched clearing whose pastoral beauty is now marred by a treacherous stretch of churned earth. Once a pristine open space, this grasping mire has become a heavy, rain-soaked trap, holding the pig family’s car prisoner in its deep, slick ruts.

places.name: The Pond
places.description: A secluded pond nestled at the field’s edge, its still waters beckon as a perfect swimming hole for weary adventurers, while the silty shallows yield a curious bounty of smooth, sun-bleached shells.

places.name: The Deep Dark Forest
places.description: A quiet, cool part of the woods where sunlight barely reaches the ground.

places.name: The Canyon
places.description: A rocky area between two steep cliffs with a trickling stream.
